column1: The [ACTIVATE PROFILE] button does not trigger profile activation or any response on the Preview profile screen
column26:
Preconditions: The user is authorized as a Recruiter.

 

Steps to reproduce:

# Open the Preview profile screen.
# Tap the [ACTIVATE PROFILE] button.
# Observe the system response and the profile status.

 

Actual result: The [ACTIVATE PROFILE] button is non-responsive upon tapping.
Expected result: After tapping the [ACTIVATE PROFILE] button, the profile status changes to "Active" or mark it on review.

Environment: iPhone 11 Pro, iOS 26.2.1, App version 1.2.6 22

column1: The user is unable to save the location in the "Desired office locations" dropdown after tapping the "Save changes" link on the  "Expectations" form.
column26:
Preconditions: 

 

Steps to reproduce:

# Open the "Expectations" form.
#  Select “Office” checkbox.
# Navigate to the "Desired office locations" dropdown.
# Tap on the field and enter a location name in the "Search" field
# Tap on the "Save changes" link that appears.
# Check if the entered location is displayed in the "Desired office locations" field.

 

Actual result: The user is redirected back to the form, but the entered location is not saved. The "Desired office locations" field remains empty
Expected result: After tapping the "Save changes" link, the user is redirected back to the form, and the entered location is successfully saved and displayed in the field.

Environment: iPhone 11 Pro, iOS 26.2.1, App version 1.2.6 22

column1: The user is unable to enter or select data in the "Select your location" dropdown on the "Expectations" form.
column26:
Preconditions: 

 

Steps to reproduce:

# Open the "Expectations" form.
# Navigate to the "Select your location" dropdown.
# Tap on the field to open the search input.
# Enter a location name into the input field.
# Observe the interface for any suggestion list or confirmation buttons.

 

Actual result: The suggestion list does not appear after typing, and there are no buttons to save or confirm the entered text. The "Select your location" field remains empty, and data cannot be submitted.
Expected result: After entering text, a suggestion list of locations appears, or some save button becomes available to confirm the entry. The selected location is successfully displayed in the field.

 

Environment: iPhone 11 Pro, iOS 26.2.1, App version 1.2.6 22

column1: The user is able to register with an invalid email format containing Cyrillic characters in the "Email" field on the "Sign up" page.

column1: [WEB] Web3 companies and Jobs
[WEB] Footer
[WEB] Profile
[WEB] Chats
column26:
Recruiters should be able to initiate a chat with a candidate on the list of candidates and on the candidate profile page.

Before contacting the candidate in the chat, the recruiter shouldn’t be able to see the contacts of the candidate.

The candidate should be able to open their contacts or decline the recruiter’s proposition and not share contact details.

The recruiter should be able to send the offer to the candidate or mark the chat as rejected.

To group chats, candidates and recruiters should have the ability to Archive chats or mark them as Favourite.

column1: [WEB] Sign In
column26: The Sign In page should have the “Sign up” link which redirects to the “Sign up” page, and the “Forgot the password” link which redirects to the “Forgot password” page.

column1: [WEB] Sign Up
[WEB] Candidates list
column26:
The list of candidates should look like a list of cards with info about a candidate. 

h3. Filters

Filters could be seen by any user, but only authorized users should be able to use them (unauthorized should be able to sign in/sign up by hovering over the filters section).

Available filters should be:

* Role;
* Technologies;
* Desired base salary (annual/monthly);
* English level;
* Candidate's location (Timezone/ Country and City).

h3. Candidates and Candidate profile

Each candidate card should contain a short description of the candidate's skills and achievements. Click on the “Show experience” should open all candidate’s experiences.

The recruiter should have the ability to start the chat with a candidate, and the candidate should be warned that he has to create a recruiter’s profile before chatting with candidates.

After clicking on the candidate’s card, the candidate’s profile should be opened in a new tab.

The candidate’s profile should contain all information about the candidate, but contacts with the candidate should be hidden before the candidate opens it (available only after the chat with a candidate).

Candidate’s experience should be sorted from the oldest to the newest positions.

column1: [WEB] Main page
column26:
h3. For companies



The main page landing. Contains info for recruiters about our product. This page should motivate recruiters to go to the list of candidates and sign up.

The page should contain a comparison with other resources, quotes from the CEO, and logos of partners.

h3. For engineers

Default main page for all users. Should contain a form for registration by email and social networks (Google, LinkedIn, GitHub), and links to Jobs and Web3 companies.

Authorized users should have a banner with the ad of the mobile app instead of a sign-up form.

At the very bottom of the page, there should be at least 10 feedbacks from real users.
